zikr_la_hawla_wala_quwwata_illa_billah: La hawla wala quwwata illa billah
zikr_subhanallahi_wa_bi_hamdih_adada_kha: Subhanallahi wa bihamdihi, 'adada khalqihi, wa rida nafsihi, wa zinata 'arshihi, wa midada kalimatihi
zikr_ya_hayyu_ya_qayyum: O Ever Living, O Self-Subsisting, by Your mercy I seek help. Correct all my affairs and do not leave me to myself for even the blink of an eye
zikr_allahumma_ajirni_minan_nar: O Allah, protect me from the Fire
zikr_la_ilaha_illa_anta_subhanaka: None has the right to be worshipped but You, Glory be to You, I have been of the wrongdoers
zikr_ya_muqallibal_qulub: O Turner of the hearts, make my heart firm upon Your religion
zikr_allahumma_innaka_afuwwun: O Allah, You are forgiving and You love forgiveness, so forgive me
zikr_raditu_billahi_rabba: I am pleased with Allah as my Lord, Islam as my religion, and Muhammad as my Prophet
zikr_allahumma_aini_ala_zhikrika: O Allah, help me to remember You, thank You, and worship You excellently
zikr_hasbi_allah_walahu_al_arsh: Allah is sufficient for me. There is no god but He. In Him I put my trust
zikr_subhan_allah_walhamdu_lillah: Glory be to Allah, All praise is for Allah, There is no god but Allah, Allah is the Greatest
zikr_ya_dhal_jalali_wal_ikram: O Possessor of Majesty and Honor
zikr_la_ilaha_illallah_al_malik: There is no god but Allah, the Sovereign, the Truth, the Manifest
zikr_astaghfirullah_lil_muminin: I seek Allah's forgiveness for the believing men and women
zikr_allahumma_maghfiratuka_awsa: O Allah, Your forgiveness is broader than my sins, and Your mercy is more hopeful to me than my deeds
zikr_allahumma_salli_ala_muhammad_full: O Allah, send blessings upon Muhammad and the family of Muhammad, as You sent blessings upon Ibrahim and the family of Ibrahim
zikr_allahumma_inni_asalukal_jannah: O Allah, I ask You for Paradise, and I seek refuge in You from the Fire
zikr_la_ilaha_illallah_al_azim_al_halim: There is no god but Allah, the Great, the Forbearing. There is no god but Allah, Lord of the Magnificent Throne

qibla_info: Find the Qibla direction from your current location
sunnah_1_text: Rinsing and Sniffing from One Handful
sunnah_1_explanation: Taking one handful of water, rinsing mouth with some, sniffing with the rest, and repeating three times.
sunnah_2_explanation: After completing wudu, say: 'I bear witness that there is no god but Allah alone with no partner, and I bear witness that Muhammad is His servant and messenger...'
sunnah_3_explanation: It is recommended for whoever performed wudu to pray two rak'ahs without thinking of worldly matters, as it is a cause for forgiveness of sins.
sunnah_4_explanation: It is recommended to vary the opening supplication. Sometimes say: 'O Allah, put distance between me and my sins...' and sometimes: 'Glory be to You, O Allah, and praise...'
sunnah_5_explanation: It is recommended to use the miswak before standing for prayer. It purifies the mouth and pleases the Lord.
sunnah_6_explanation: The praying person places something in front of them (a wall, pillar, or raised object) to prevent people from passing in front. This is an emphasized sunnah.
sunnah_7_explanation: A very brief sitting the praying person does after the second prostration of the first and third rak'ah before standing.
sunnah_8_explanation: Raising the feet and sitting on the heels between prostrations, done occasionally as a sunnah.
sunnah_9_source: Narrated by Abu Dawud and Tirmidhi
sunnah_9_explanation: A Muslim prostrates once praising and thanking Allah when receiving a new blessing or being saved from a calamity.
sunnah_10_explanation: Dusting the bed with the inside of the garment three times, because one does not know what came upon the bed.
sunnah_11_text: Sleeping in a State of Purity
sunnah_11_explanation: It is recommended for a Muslim to perform wudu before sleeping.
sunnah_12_source: Narrated by Abu Dawud and Tirmidhi
sunnah_12_explanation: It protects from the punishment of the grave, and it is recommended to read it every night.
sunnah_13_explanation: The sunnah is to lick the fingers before wiping or washing them, for there is a blessing in them that one does not know which part of the food it is in.
sunnah_14_text: Praising Allah After Eating and Drinking
sunnah_14_explanation: Say: 'Praise be to Allah who fed us, gave us drink, and made us Muslims', or similar phrases of praise.
sunnah_15_text: Breathing Outside the Vessel While Drinking
sunnah_15_explanation: Drink water in three sips, breathing outside the vessel between each sip.
sunnah_16_explanation: To be the first to initiate the greeting upon meeting, and the better of them is the one who initiates the greeting.
sunnah_17_explanation: Spreading and giving salam to those you know and don't know among the Muslims.
sunnah_18_explanation: Showing cheerfulness and smiling when meeting a Muslim is a righteous deed that earns reward.
sunnah_19_source: Narrated by Abu Dawud and Tirmidhi
sunnah_19_explanation: Muslims shaking hands causes sins to fall like leaves fall from trees.
sunnah_20_explanation: It is recommended for men to visit graves to remember the afterlife and pray for the deceased.
sunnah_21_explanation: At minimum two rak'ahs, its time is from when the sun rises a spear's length to just before noon. It is the prayer of the frequently repentant.
sunnah_22_explanation: Ending the night prayer with one rak'ah. It is an emphasized sunnah.
sunnah_23_source: Narrated by Ahmad and Abu Dawud
sunnah_23_explanation: It is recommended to walk without shoes occasionally as humility to Allah and self-discipline, in suitable places.
sunnah_24_explanation: Whoever has something to bequeath should not let two nights pass without having his will written at his bedside.
sunnah_25_explanation: It is recommended to expose part of the body to the rain and say: 'O Allah, send beneficial rain.'
sunnah_26_explanation: Preventing children from going out at sunset (when devils spread) then releasing them after one hour of the night has passed.
